Container.Name: brave-browser
Container.Overview:
Brave is a free and open-source web browser focused on privacy and security. It includes a built-in ad blocker, tracker blocking, and fingerprinting protection by default — no extensions required.
The graphical user interface (GUI) of the application can be accessed through a modern web browser, requiring no installation or configuration on the client side, or via any VNC client.
This container is built on jlesage/baseimage-gui using TigerVNC, providing a significantly more responsive experience than containers using WebRTC-based streaming technologies.
For a complete documentation of this container, see https://github.com/ESClaus/docker-brave#readme
Container.Description:
Brave is a free and open-source web browser focused on privacy and security. It includes a built-in ad blocker, tracker blocking, and fingerprinting protection by default — no extensions required.
The graphical user interface (GUI) of the application can be accessed through a modern web browser, requiring no installation or configuration on the client side, or via any VNC client.
This container is built on jlesage/baseimage-gui using TigerVNC, providing a significantly more responsive experience than containers using WebRTC-based streaming technologies.
For a complete documentation, visit https://github.com/ESClaus/docker-brave/blob/main/README.md.

Config.Name: Config Directory
Config.Description: Stores the application's configuration, state, logs, and any files requiring persistency.

Config.Name: User ID
Config.Description: ID of the user the application runs as.

Config.Name: Group ID
Config.Description: ID of the group the application runs as.

Config.Name: Umask
Config.Description: Mask controlling permissions for newly created files and folders, specified in octal notation.

Config.Name: Display Width
Config.Description: Width (in pixels) of the application's window.

Config.Name: Display Height
Config.Description: Height (in pixels) of the application's window.

Config.Name: Dark Mode
Config.Description: When set to 1, enables dark mode for the application.

Config.Name: Web Audio
Config.Description: When set to 1, enables audio support, allowing audio produced by the application to play through the browser.

Config.Name: Web File Manager
Config.Description: When set to 1, enables the web file manager, allowing interaction with files inside the container through a web browser.

Config.Name: Web File Manager Allowed Paths
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of paths within the container that the file manager can access.

Config.Name: Web File Manager Denied Paths
Config.Description: Comma-separated list of paths within the container that the file manager cannot access.

Config.Name: Web Desktop Notifications
Config.Description: When set to 1, enables the web notification service, allowing the browser to display desktop notifications from the application.

Config.Name: Web Terminal
Config.Description: When set to 1, enables access to a terminal from the web interface.

Config.Name: Web Authentication
Config.Description: When set to 1, protects the application's GUI with a login page when accessed via a web browser.

Config.Name: Web Authentication Token Validity Time
Config.Description: Lifetime of a token in hours. A token is assigned after successful login.

Config.Name: Web Authentication Username
Config.Description: Optional username for web authentication.

Config.Name: Web Authentication Password
Config.Description: Optional password for web authentication.

Config.Name: Secure Connection
Config.Description: When set to 1, uses an encrypted connection to access the application's GUI via web browser or VNC client.

Config.Name: Secure Connection VNC Method
Config.Description: Method used for encrypted VNC connection. Possible values are SSL or TLS.

Config.Name: Secure Connection Certificate Check Interval
Config.Description: Interval in seconds at which the system checks if web or VNC certificates have changed.

Config.Name: Web Listening Port (Non-Bridge Network Type)
Config.Description: Port used by the web server to serve the application's GUI. NOTE: Relevant only when Network Type is not Bridge.

Config.Name: VNC Listening Port (Non-Bridge Network Type)
Config.Description: Port used by the VNC server to serve the application's GUI. NOTE: Relevant only when Network Type is not Bridge.

Config.Name: Open URL
Config.Description: The URL to open when Brave starts. Multiple URLs can be opened by separating them with the pipe character (|).

Config.Name: Kiosk Mode
Config.Description: Set to 1 to enable kiosk mode. Launches Brave in a restricted full-screen mode suitable for public displays.

Config.Name: Custom Arguments
Config.Description: Custom argument(s) to pass when launching Brave.

Config.Name: WEB Port for GUI (Bridge Network Type)
Config.Description: Port to access the application's GUI via the web interface. NOTE: Relevant only when Network Type is set to Bridge.

Config.Name: VNC Port for GUI (Bridge Network Type)
Config.Description: Port to access the application's GUI via VNC protocol. Optional if no VNC client is used. NOTE: Relevant only when Network Type is set to Bridge.
